The loop conditions use the incorrect relational operator．
The outer and inner for loops contain different Boolean expressions．
The loop control variable increments $a++$ and $b++$ are incorrect．
The row and column indices in the print method call are reversed．

questions.prompt:
The following method prints the value of start and then prints every other value up to，and possibly including，end．For example，the method call printRun（1，7）prints the values 1，3，5，and 7.

Consider a modification to the method that changes the Boolean expression in the for loop header so that the for loop header becomes the following．

Which of the following best explains how the output of the modified method differs from the output of the original method？

options.content: The modified method always prints fewer values than the original method．
The modified method prints fewer values than the original method only when start is even．
The modified method prints fewer values than the original method only when start and end are both even or when start and end are both odd．
The modified method prints fewer values than the original method only when start is even and end is odd or when start is odd and end is even．
The modified method never prints fewer values than the original method．

COMPUTER SCIENCE A
SECTION II
Time－1 hour and 30 minutes
4 Questions



Directions：SHOW ALL YOUR WORK．REMEMBER THAT PROGRAM SEGMENTS ARE TO BE
WRITTEN IN JAVA．You may plan your answers in this orange booklet，but no credit will be given for anything written in this booklet．You will only earn credit for what you write in the separate Free Response booklet．

Notes：
－Assume that the classes listed in the Java Quick Reference have been imported where appropriate．
－Unless otherwise noted in the question，assume that parameters in method calls are not null and that methods are called only when their preconditions are satisfied．
－In writing solutions for each question，you may use any of the accessible methods that are listed in classes defined in that question．Writing significant amounts of code that can be replaced by a call to one of these methods will not receive full credit．

NO TEST MATERIAL ON THIS PAGE
